atoms.source_snippet:
[package]
name = "ritual-tee-registry-reader"
version = "0.1.0"
edition = "2021"
license = "MIT OR Apache-2.0"
repository = "https://github.com/ritual-net/ritual-reth-internal"
description = "Unified TEEServiceRegistry contract reader for Ritual Network"
...
[dependencies]
alloy-primitives.workspace = true
revm.workspace = true
reth-storage-api.workspace = true
reth-revm.workspace = true
thiserror.workspace = true
tracing.workspace = true
atoms.semantic_claim: The old divergence adds the TEE registry reader crate manifest with dependencies needed for raw TEEServiceRegistry storage reads through REVM database/state-provider abstractions, Alloy primitives, tracing, and typed errors.

destinations.evidence: Destination preserves the crate name, description, dependency set, and storage-reader role while normalizing package version/edition/rust-version/license/repository/lints to workspace values and enabling `revm` std feature through workspace form.

atoms.equivalence_argument: The destination manifest keeps the same build capabilities under v2.2.0 workspace conventions.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Removing `reth-revm` should break the `StateProviderDatabase` bridge import.

summary: The TEE registry reader manifest is structurally equivalent in the v2.2.0 port with workspace-normalized metadata and dependencies.
